newsBriefing: Liverpool answered their recent league stumble at Molineux with a decisive 3-1 FA Cup win over Wolves, powered by Andy Robertson's goal-and-assist performance to book a quarter-final spot.

games.headline: Robertson leads Liverpool's cup response as Reds knock out Wolves
games.brief: Liverpool got the exact response they needed, beating Wolves 3-1 at Molineux to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals just days after losing to the same opponent in the league. Andy Robertson changed the tie with a rocket from range and then set up Mohamed Salah moments later, flipping a cagey game into Liverpool's control. Curtis Jones added a classy third to finish it off before a late Hee-Chan Hwang consolation, giving Arne Slot's side momentum ahead of a busy week in Europe.
bullets: Liverpool won 3-1 away to Wolves to advance to the FA Cup quarter-finals.
Andy Robertson scored and assisted within the early second-half burst that decided the tie.
Mohamed Salah's goal was initially flagged offside before VAR awarded it.
Curtis Jones scored Liverpool's third after Slot's side controlled the second half.
Wolves had only one big late payoff chance and scored through Hee-Chan Hwang, but never recovered from conceding twice in quick succession.
games.context: The win avenges Tuesday's stoppage-time league defeat at Molineux and sends Liverpool into the last-eight draw with confidence restored.
